item.title: Fiction writer Afsana Begum reappointed director of Jatiya Grontha Kendra
item.description: Fiction writer Afsana Begum has been reappointed as the director of the Jatiya Grontha Kendra also known as National Book Centre for a one-year term, following her sudden removal from the office on January 20....

item.title: Left parties to wage tougher movement over US trade deal
item.description: Leaders of the Anti-Fascist and Anti-War Alliance on Sunday, at a protest rally in Dhaka, called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to scrap the ‘unequal’ trade deal signed with the United States government during the rule of interim government....

item.title: Thyroid conference held in city
item.description: The Thyroid Center Ltd and the Bangladesh Institute of Thyroid Medicine and Imaging Research (BITMIR) jointly organised a thyroid conference titled ‘Thyroid-The Mother Gland’ for specialist physicians at the campus of The Thyroid Center and BITMIR at Dhanmondi on Saturday....Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.description: Entrepreneurs’ Organisation Bangladesh has organised a workshop titled Learning Presents Leading with AI: Innovation, Strategy and Growth at a hotel in Dhaka....

item.title: NBR plans taxing motorcycles, auto-rickshaws in budget
item.description: The National Board of Revenue is considering imposing advance income tax on motorcycles and battery-run rickshaws in the proposed budget for the 2026–27 fiscal year as part of efforts to widen the country’s tax net and increase revenue collection......

item.title: Central bank finds efficiency gaps in one-fifth units
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has introduced a monthly performance review system after finding that nearly one-fifth of its departments and branch offices had significant efficiency gaps in complying with official service delivery timelines......

item.title: Tk 552cr ‘looted’ at Monipur School and College in 16 years
item.description:
Monipur High School and College, a renowned educational institution at Mirpur in the capital Dhaka, allegedly witnessed unprecedented looting, fraud and administrative misconduct over the past 16 years during the ousted Awami League regime, according to a government probe report.
...

item.title: New Zealand mulls continuing benefits after Bangladesh’s LDC graduation
item.description: New Zealand has expressed interest in continuing duty-free and preferential market access facilities for Bangladesh even after its graduation from the least developed country category, while also seeking to expand bilateral trade and investment between the two countries......Geographic Reference

item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon as Hezbollah calls talks ‘dead end’
item.description: Israel struck eastern and southern Lebanon on Sunday, state media reported, despite a fragile ceasefire as Hezbollah called US-brokered talks between the two countries a ‘dead end’....

item.title: Airbus submits fresh 10-jet offer to Biman
item.description: European aerospace giant Airbus has made a fresh proposal to Biman Bangladesh Airlines to supply 10 aircraft, days after the national carrier signed a $3.7 billion deal with rival Boeing, officials said today......

item.title: DSE, Swisscontact to boost SME participation
item.description: The Dhaka Stock Exchange PLC and Swisscontact Bangladesh signed a memorandum of understanding on Sunday aimed at strengthening the SME sector in the capital market and advancing sustainable, inclusive economic development through the country’s securities ecosystem......

item.title: Govt plans to turn leather sector into major income source
item.description: Industries, commerce, and textiles and jute minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir on Sunday said that the government had undertaken comprehensive short and long-term initiatives for the modernization and sustainable development of the country’s leather sector with the aim of transforming it into a major source of national income......

item.title: Stocks slide back into red zone
item.description: Stocks on Sunday reverted to correction territory as persistent selling pressure on major scrips erased the modest recovery seen over the previous three trading sessions......

item.title: India scrambles to steady rupee as oil shock bites
item.description: India is scrambling to salvage a sinking rupee as surging oil prices linked to the Middle East conflict threaten to disrupt the world’s fastest-growing major economy......

item.title: Risks, rewards in upcoming IPOs at SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic
item.description: Wall Street is licking its chops over an unprecedented slate of massive IPOs set to arrive in the coming months, beginning with Elon Musk’s SpaceX in June......

item.description: Sonali Investment PLC on Sunday held its 16th annual general meeting at Sonali Bank PLC head office in the capital, said a press release......Press Release Distribution

item.description: A youth was beaten to death allegedly by smugglers at Tahirpur border in Sunamganj early Sunday....

item.title: Drone strike starts fire outside UAE nuclear plant
item.description: A drone strike triggered a fire near a nuclear power station in the emirate of Abu Dhabi on Sunday, authorities said, reporting no injuries or impact on radiation levels....

item.title: Rice prices rise in Naogaon
item.description: Rice prices have increased on both wholesale and retail markets in Naogaon, defying expectations during the peak Boro harvesting season when prices usually remain stable or decline......

item.title: German ‘chemical town’ fears impact of industrial decline
item.description: Germany’s industrial decline is taking a painful toll on communities that have long relied on local manufacturing titans for jobs, prosperity and a sense of a secure future......

item.title: Scuffles from Europe to NYC as Swatch sale descends into chaos
item.description: The launch of limited edition Swatch watches descended into chaos Saturday in several European cities and New York, with French police firing tear gas to restore order at one store near Paris......

item.title: HC order on interim govt legality May 21
item.description: The High Court on Sunday set May 21 for passing its order on the public-interest-litigation writ petition that sought the government’s explanation why it should not be directed to form an independent commission to investigate the alleged......

item.description: Hundreds of companies raised a combined $70 billion by selling shares to the public in the United States last year......

item.title: US made no tangible concessions in proposal response, says Iran
item.description: Iranian media said Sunday that the United States had failed to make any concrete concessions in its latest response to Iran’s proposed agenda for negotiations to end the war....

item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission has launched an inquiry into allegations of embezzlement of nearly Tk 18,000 crore from Union Bank PLC under the guise of loans....

item.description: Yadea on Sunday launched its new electric vehicle model GT25 at its flagship showroom at Tejgaon in the capital, said a press release......

item.description: Experts at a discussion on Sunday suggested reducing duties on renewable energy equipment, strengthening power transmission lines and increasing government support for the renewable energy sector boost....

item.description: Saiful Islam on Sunday officially joined Rupali Bank PLC’s board as director during the board’s meeting, said a press release......

item.title: DBBL, Berger Paints launch Mastercard cards
item.description: Dutch-Bangla Bank PLC and Berger Paints Bangladesh Limited have recently collaborated with Mastercard to launch a new range of co-branded debit and prepaid cards for Berger dealers and painters, said a press release......

item.title: MTB Capital arranger of Techno Drugs’ Tk 500m bond
item.description: Techno Drugs Limited approved issuance of a convertible, redeemable bond at its 196th board meeting on May 14, said a press release......Press Release Distribution

item.title: Qoumi education needs regulatory oversight
item.description: QOUMI madrassahs running outside any regulatory purview has raised some concern. The proposition prevents the government from ensuring the safety of the students of such institutions, their education and their mental and physical health. Besides, there is the problem of their integration into a productive work force. The concern becomes grave as the number......

item.title: Worrying collapse of medical ethics in public hospitals
item.description: THE death of a patient at Sher-e-Bangla Medical College Hospital on May 13 after a trolley man allegedly removed her oxygen support for another patient whose family had paid Tk 200 in bribe reveals the horrifying state of public healthcare services. The allegation that a hospital staff member disconnected oxygen support from a critically ill woman, struggling to breathe......

item.title: Economics of biodiversity
item.description: BANGLADESH seldom frames biodiversity in economic terms, yet its rivers, wetlands, forests and coastal ecosystems sustain livelihoods on a vast scale every day. From the Sundarbans shielding coastal districts against cyclones to the haor basins supporting fisheries and agriculture, biodiversity is not simply an environmental concern; it is a foundational pillar of the......

item.title: Cultural decline of reading spaces
item.description: READING spaces have long played a vital role in shaping Bangladesh’s intellectual and cultural identity. Public libraries, community reading rooms, bookshops, book cafés and even informal neighbourhood spaces once served as vibrant centres for knowledge exchange, critical thinking and cultural engagement. From the 1952 language movement onward, reading......

item.title: Can AI cameras rescue Dhaka’s chaotic roads?
item.description: EVERY morning in Dhaka feels less like a commute and more like a test of endurance. A university student waits endlessly for a bus that appears trapped somewhere inside the city’s endless gridlock. An office worker studies maps like a battlefield strategist, searching for shortcuts that might save a few precious minutes. Inside an ambulance, a patient measures......

item.title: When art schools fail their students
item.description: THE tragic death of a student associated with the theatre and performance studies department at University of Dhaka has opened a wound that extends far beyond a single campus. What followed the incident was not merely grief, but an extraordinary outpouring of testimonies from former and current students describing years of humiliation, fear, emotional......

item.title: Pakistan offers free higher education to 500 Bangladeshi students
item.description: Around 500 Bangladeshi students will get the opportunity to pursue higher education free of cost at different universities in Pakistan under the Bangladesh-Pakistan Knowledge Corridor initiative....Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: Ex-BNP secy gen Delwar’s son ‘grabs’ president post
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s former secretary general Khandaker Delwar Hossain’s son, Khandaker Akhter Hamid Khan Paban, allegedly grabbed the post of president of the capital’s Motalib Plaza Shop Owners’ Association without taking part in an election on Saturday evening....

item.title: HADI MURDER: Further probe report submission deferred for 13th time
item.description: An additional chief metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Sunday extended the deadline for submitting the further investigation report in the case of the killing of Inquilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi by 21 more days....

item.title: 33 ‘smugglers’ arrested while smuggling cement to Myanmar
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard outpost Shahpari and the Bangladesh Navy in a joint operation on Saturday arrested 33 smugglers along with 2,400 bags of cement and three boats while smuggling to Myanmar....

item.title: Explain failure to protect children’s rights: HC
item.description: The High Court on Sunday issued a rule asking the authorities concerned to explain why the failure to take effective steps to protect and ensure the fundamental rights of street children should not be declared illegal....

item.title: RAB arrests three with counterfeit notes in Chattogram
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion arrested three people with counterfeit currency worth Tk 1,13,600 during separate drives in Chattogram’s Panchlaish and Hathazari areas on Saturday....City & Local Guides

item.description: Dubai Taxi Company will recruit 6,000 skilled drivers from Bangladesh this year....

item.title: Spacecraft to probe how Earth fends off raging solar winds
item.description: A joint European-Chinese spacecraft is set to blast off Tuesday to investigate what happens when extreme winds and giant explosions of plasma shot out from the Sun slam into Earth’s magnetic shield....

item.title: Hirja set on fire dies at DMCH after 4 days
item.description: A Hijra community member, who was set on fire allegedly by fellows over disputes regarding money matters in Cumilla, died on Sunday afternoon while undergoing treatment at Dhaka Medical College Hospital in the capital....

item.description: The West Bengal of India has sped up barbed-wire fencing along the North 24 Parganas border with Bangladesh, reported The Times of India on Sunday....

item.title: Mass Ukrainian drone attack kills four in Russia
item.description: A wave of almost 600 Ukrainian drones attacked Russia overnight killing four people, authorities said on Sunday, as Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky called the barrage an ‘entirely justified’ retribution for Moscow’s own pummelling of Ukraine....

item.title: Former foreign ministry DG Sabbir arrested at Patgram border
item.description: Former foreign ministry director general Md Sabbir Bin Shams, who was detained at the Angarpota border at Patgram upazila in Lalmonirhat on Saturday afternoon while allegedly entering Bangladesh from India illegally, was sent to jail on Sunday....Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.description: The exchange of allegations and counter-allegations between the government and the opposition over implementing the July National Charter and the referendum outcome has finally moved into thepoliticalfield....

item.description: Two brothers drowned in a pond at Paniabanda village of ​​Porabari Union in Tangail Sadar Upazila at about 2:00pm on Sunday....

item.title: Sylhet hawkers stage sit-in protesting at eviction from streets
item.description: Hawkers in Sylhet staged a sit-in, blocking the busy road in front of Sylhet Nagar Bhaban on Sunday to demand an immediate end to harassment against them and approval to sit on the city streets with their businesses....

item.title: Pregnant woman slaughtered over family dispute in Satkhira
item.description: A four-month pregnant woman was allegedly slaughtered by her husband following a family dispute in Satkhira sadar upazila late on Saturday night....Politics

item.description: A threatening note demanding Tk 5 lakh, along with a bomb-like object and a shroud, was found outside a farmer’s home at Meherpur’s Gangni upazila, causing fear among local residents....

item.description: Chattogram district superintendent of police MD Masud Alam on Sunday stated that the force would follow a zero-tolerance policy against criminals to make Chattogram free from crimes....

item.title: Three put on remand, protest at Shah Ali shrine attack on
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday placed three arrested on two-day police remand in the case filed over the recent attack on devotees at a shrine in Mirpur area of the capital Dhaka....
